name: Balcó de la Fontpobra Loop from Sant Feliu de Pallerols
name_original: Parque Natural de la Garrotxa – circular desde Sant Feliu de Pallerols por el Balcó de la Fontpobra
description: This moderate 17.5-mile mountain biking loop in Garrotxa Natural Park offers volcanic scenery and panoramic views from Balcó de la Fontpobra
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Sant Feliu de Pallerols traverse the unique volcanic landscapes of the La Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park. The region features varied terrain, including ancient volcanoes, lush forests, and river valleys. Mountain bikers can expect a mix of challenging sections, natural rock gardens, and flowy paths, providing diverse riding experiences. The area's geological makeup, with features like the Enchanted Rocks and the Brugent river, contributes to a rich environment for outdoor activities. Best mountain bike trails around Sant Feliu de Pallerols The most popular mountain bike trail is Balcó de la Fontpobra Loop from Sant Feliu de Pallerols, a 17.5 miles (28.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 47 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the Fontpobra volcano and surrounding volcanic terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Ruta del Carrilet Greenway – Station Path loop from Sant Feliu de Pallerols, a moderate 17.3 miles (27.8 km) path. This trail follows sections of the historic Carrilet Greenway, offering a smoother ride through the valley. Local mountain bikers also love the Col de Bas – View of Els Hostalets d'en Bas loop from Sant Feliu de Pallerols, a 20.9 miles (33.7 km) trail leading through the Hostoles Valley, often completed in about 2 hours 50 minutes. Mountain biking around Sant Feliu de Pallerols is defined by volcanic hills, dense forests, and river valleys. The extensive network of routes offers options for various ability levels, from easier greenway sections to more challenging technical trails. The routes in Sant Feliu de Pallerols are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 170 reviews. More than 3000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Sant Feliu de Pallerols's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Sant Feliu de Pallerols?
A: There are over 200 mountain bike trails around Sant Feliu de Pallerols. This includes a wide variety of routes, with 22 easy, 83 moderate, and 101 difficult options, ensuring there's something for every skill level.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Sant Feliu de Pallerols?
A: You can expect a diverse range of terrain, from the unique volcanic landscapes of the La Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park to lush forests and river valleys. Trails feature a mix of challenging sections, natural rock gardens, and flowy paths, often winding through ancient volcanoes like Sant Marc and Puig Roig, and past geological formations such as the Enchanted Rocks.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Sant Feliu de Pallerols?
A: Yes, there are 22 easy mountain bike trails in the area. For a smoother ride, consider routes that incorporate sections of the historic Carrilet Greenway, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31558513" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta del Carrilet Greenway – Station Path loop from Sant Feliu de Pallerols&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate but less technical experience through the valley.

Q: What are some notable natural features or viewpoints I can see while mountain biking?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can ride past volcanic formations like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1134337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Margarida Volcano&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4052842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Croscat Volcano Scoria Slopes&lt;/a&gt;. The unique 'Enchanted Rocks' (Les Roques Encantades) are also a prominent feature on some trails. For panoramic views, consider routes that pass by viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4666614" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Osca de Cabrera Rocky Ridge&lt;/a&gt; or offer vistas of the Hostoles Valley.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Sant Feliu de Pallerols?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939420163" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcó de la Fontpobra Loop from Sant Feliu de Pallerols&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular trail offering views of the Fontpobra volcano. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24556645" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de Bas – View of Els Hostalets d'en Bas loop from Sant Feliu de Pallerols&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the scenic Hostoles Valley.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Sant Feliu de Pallerols?
A: The region generally offers good conditions for mountain biking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, but the lush forests offer shade. Winter rides are possible, though some higher or more exposed sections might be cooler or have varying conditions.

Q: Can I find challenging mountain bike trails for advanced riders?
A: Absolutely. Sant Feliu de Pallerols offers 101 difficult mountain bike trails. These routes often feature significant elevation gains and technical sections, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33844539" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font Pobra – Santa Maria de Finestres loop from Sant Feliu de Pallerols&lt;/a&gt;, which includes over 1300 meters of elevation gain over 48 kilometers.

Q: Is there parking available near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, Sant Feliu de Pallerols, as a hub for outdoor activities, typically offers parking options within or near the village center, providing convenient access to the trailheads. Specific parking details can often be found on individual route descriptions.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Sant Feliu de Pallerols?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 170 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse volcanic landscapes, the variety of terrain from flowy paths to technical sections, and the extensive network of trails that cater to different abilities.

Q: Are there any specific singletracks or bike park areas nearby for mountain bikers?
A: Yes, for those seeking dedicated singletrack experiences, there are several options nearby. You can explore highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6550960" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Rebolled Singletrack&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5563875" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Crest Line Singletrack&lt;/a&gt;, or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5765223" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bonavista Singletrack&lt;/a&gt;, which are part of the broader network of bike parks and trails in the region.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that incorporate the Carrilet Greenway?
A: Yes, the Carrilet Greenway is a significant feature in the area. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31558513" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta del Carrilet Greenway – Station Path loop from Sant Feliu de Pallerols&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17567096" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Sant Esteve – Ruta del Carrilet Greenway loop from Sant Feliu de Pallerols&lt;/a&gt; utilize sections of this historic greenway, offering a generally smoother and more accessible riding experience.
tips: Spectacular views of the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park
A meadow not very big but curious to see for its landscape and large abandoned farmhouses, most of them demolished.

A viewpoint with spectacular views of La Garrotxa. At the top there are three crosses in memory of the old oratory that existed in Puig de les Tres Creus after it was destroyed and no written document or image of it could be recovered.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This 28.2 km mountain bike route, with an elevation gain of 740 meters, usually takes around 2 hours and 47 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is classified as moderate and requires good fitness and advanced riding skills. It corresponds approximately to an S2 rating on the Single Track Scale, meaning you can expect some technical sections with obstacles like roots and rocks. It's best suited for experienced mountain bikers.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, reflecting the volcanic geology of the La Garrotxa region. You'll encounter compacted earth, gravel paths, and potentially sections with volcanic rock or loose stones. The route also passes through lush forests, offering a diverse riding experience.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers picturesque and diverse landscapes within the La Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park. Key highlights include the Coll de Fontpobra, the stunning Fontpobra Balcony with its panoramic views, Pla de les Medes, and Les Tres Creus (744m).

faqs.question: Are there any specific regulations or permits needed for mountain biking in La Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park?
faqs.answer: The La Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park generally does not require specific permits for mountain biking on marked trails. However, it's crucial to respect park regulations, which include staying on designated paths, not disturbing wildlife, and practicing Leave No Trace principles. There are no entrance fees for the park itself.

faqs.question: Where can I park or access public transport to reach the trailhead in Sant Feliu de Pallerols?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Sant Feliu de Pallerols. While specific parking details for this exact trailhead are not provided, Sant Feliu de Pallerols is a small town, and you can typically find public parking areas. For public transport, regional bus services connect Sant Feliu de Pallerols with larger towns in the Girona province, though schedules may be limited.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the La Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park generally allows dogs, it's important to keep them on a leash at all times to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always clean up after your pet. Check local signage for any specific restrictions on this particular route.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to mountain bike in the La Garrotxa region is typically during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is usually mild and pleasant, and the natural scenery is particularly vibrant. Summers can be hot, and winters might bring colder temperatures or occasional rain.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other official trails. Notably, it covers parts of the Volta a peu a la Garrotxa (22%), PirineXus (18%), and the Ruta del Carrilet I: "Girona-Olot" (18%), which is also known as the Via Verda Carrilet Olot - Girona. You'll also find smaller sections overlapping with trails like Camí de les Encies a les Medes and Camí de Sant Feliu de Pallerols a Sant Pau.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation in Sant Feliu de Pallerols?
faqs.answer: Sant Feliu de Pallerols is a charming town and offers basic amenities. You can expect to find local cafes, restaurants, and some accommodation options, such as guesthouses or rural hotels, making it a convenient base for exploring the area before or after your ride.
